section13_checks.prohibition_description: Treating Grant Agreement Annex templates as the governing structural schema for proposal writing.
section13_checks.check_rationale: The artifact's schema_id is 'orch.tier5.implementation_section.v1', a canonical Tier 5 application-form section schema. The traceability_footer.primary_sources[] references 'docs/tier2a_instrument_schemas/extracted/section_schema_registry.json' and 'docs/tier2a_instrument_schemas/extracted/evaluator_expectation_registry.json' as structural authorities — both are Tier 2A instrument schema sources, not Grant Agreement Annex templates. No string matching 'Annex [0-9]', 'Grant Agreement Annex', 'AGA Template', or 'Model Grant Agreement template' used as a structural authority was found anywhere in the artifact content, sub-sections, or traceability footer.

section13_checks.prohibition_description: Inventing call constraints, scope requirements, expected outcomes, or expected impacts not present in Tier 2B source documents.
section13_checks.check_rationale: The artifact's B.3.2 sub-section references call constraints and scope requirements by identifiers: 'SR-09, CC-03, and CC-04' (ATU coordination activities), 'SR-10 and CC-05' (BAL AI-on-demand platform integration per claim C12), and 'CC-07' (FIIT Testing and Experiment Facility coordination). All such references are confirmed in validation_status.claim_statuses (C12: confirmed, source_ref = 'Tier 2B: scope_requirements.json SR-10; call_constraints.json CC-05'). The traceability_footer.primary_sources[] includes 'docs/tier2b_topic_and_call_sources/extracted/scope_requirements.json' (tier: 2) and 'docs/tier2b_topic_and_call_sources/extracted/call_constraints.json' (tier: 2) — both within docs/tier2b_topic_and_call_sources/extracted/. All call-constraint and scope-requirement claims in the artifact carry a Tier 2B source reference in the traceability footer.

section13_checks.prohibition_description: Inventing project facts — partner names, capabilities, roles, objectives, prior experience, budget figures, team sizes, equipment — not present in Tier 3.
section13_checks.check_rationale: The artifact's B.3.2 sub-section names eight specific partners (ATU, CERIA, BIIS, ISIA, NIHS, ELI, BAL, FIIT) with detailed capability and role descriptions. The traceability_footer.primary_sources[] includes 'docs/tier3_project_instantiation/consortium/partners.json' (tier: 3) and 'docs/tier3_project_instantiation/consortium/roles.json' (tier: 3), both paths within docs/tier3_project_instantiation/. Claim_statuses confirm: C09 ('Partner legal names, countries, and organisation types', confirmed, Tier 3 consortium/partners.json), C10 ('NIHS provides de-identified clinical datasets and clinical validation pathways', confirmed, Tier 3 partners.json[NIHS].description), C11 ('ELI provides real-world logistics operations data across international corridors', confirmed, Tier 3 partners.json[ELI].description), C08 ('ATU leads both WP1 and WP2', confirmed, Tier 4 wp_structure.json). Equipment claims (ISIA 'operational pilot factory environment') and all partner capability descriptions are grounded under the partners.json Tier 3 source reference in the traceability footer. No project-fact claim is found to carry zero Tier 3 source reference. Note: this skill does not independently read docs/tier3_project_instantiation/; it verifies that the artifact provides a Tier 3 source reference in traceability_footer.primary_sources[], which it does.

section13_checks.prohibition_description: Commencing any Phase 8 activity before the budget gate (Phase 7) has passed.
section13_checks.check_rationale: This is a Phase 8 artifact (schema_id 'orch.tier5.implementation_section.v1' begins with 'orch.tier5.'). The artifact's validation_status.claim_statuses includes C15: 'Budget gate passed; all 9 WPs and 8 partners confirmed in validated budget', status 'confirmed', source_ref 'Tier 4: phase7_budget_gate/budget_gate_assessment.json gate_pass_declaration=pass'. The traceability_footer.primary_sources[] includes 'docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase7_budget_gate/budget_gate_assessment.json' (tier: 4), confirming budget gate awareness. Scan of B.3.1 and B.3.2 narrative content: no budget figures matching patterns '€[0-9]+', '[0-9]+ EUR', or '[0-9]+ person-months' used as confirmed budget values were found. Duration references ('48 months', 'M3', 'M24', 'M30', 'M36', 'M42', 'M48') are timeline references, not budget figures. The artifact does not contain budget-dependent content without gate evidence, and it asserts gate passage via a confirmed Tier 4 claim.

section13_checks.prohibition_description: Storing durable decisions only in agent memory without writing them to Tier 4.
section13_checks.check_rationale: Not directly checkable by reading an artifact's content — this prohibition applies to the runtime execution behaviour of agents, not to the content of the deliverable artifact itself. No artifact-level evidence of this violation type exists or can be detected from the implementation_section.json artifact. Check recorded as pass per skill specification §2 (checks 13.5, 13.6, 13.11, 13.12 are structurally non-applicable to artifact-level content inspection and are recorded as pass with explicit non-applicability reason).

section13_checks.prohibition_description: Allowing a workflow or skill to become a de facto constitutional authority by expanding its scope to redefine phase meanings, tier meanings, or gate logic.
section13_checks.check_rationale: Not directly checkable from a deliverable artifact. This prohibition applies to the definition and runtime behaviour of workflows and skills, not to proposal section content. The implementation_section.json artifact is a Tier 5 proposal section; it neither defines nor redefines phase meanings, tier meanings, or gate logic. Check recorded as pass per skill specification §2 non-applicability rule.

section13_checks.prohibition_description: Silently reordering phases or weakening gate conditions to allow workflow progress when inputs are incomplete.
section13_checks.check_rationale: This is a Phase 8 artifact (the final phase). The artifact's traceability_footer.primary_sources[] references outputs exclusively from upstream phases: Phase 3 (wp_structure.json), Phase 4 (gantt.json), Phase 6 (implementation_architecture.json), Phase 7 (budget_gate_assessment.json), plus Tier 3 and Tier 2 sources. All referenced phase outputs are from phases that precede Phase 8 in the canonical sequence (Phases 3→4→6→7→8). No reference to a downstream phase output, no skipped phase gap, and no self-referential phase ordering anomaly is detected in the artifact. Phase sequencing is consistent with the canonical workflow.

section13_checks.prohibition_description: Finalizing proposal text when source state is known to be incomplete, contradictory, or unvalidated, without explicitly flagging the gap in the deliverable.
section13_checks.check_rationale: Applicable to Tier 5 deliverable artifacts. The artifact's validation_status.overall_status is 'inferred' — not 'unresolved'. Review of all 15 claim_statuses entries (C01–C15) confirms: 13 entries carry status 'confirmed', 2 entries carry status 'inferred' (C13 and C14, both with stated source_refs to Tier 4 implementation_architecture.json ethics_assessment). No entry carries status 'unresolved' or 'assumed'. Because overall_status is not 'unresolved' and no individual claim is unresolved, the trigger condition for this check (unresolved content without an explicit '[DATA REQUIRED]' or 'INCOMPLETE:' flag) is not met. The inferred claims (C13, C14) provide stated inference sources, consistent with §12.2 Inferred category requirements.

section13_checks.prohibition_description: Using agent-local knowledge of Horizon Europe programme rules as a substitute for reading Tier 1 source documents when those documents are present and accessible.
section13_checks.check_rationale: The artifact's content (B.3.1, B.3.2) does not contain explicit claims asserting Horizon Europe programme rules — no participation conditions, grant agreement terms, or financial regulation requirements are cited as governing authorities in the text. The ethics references in B.3.1 ('ethics compliance oversight', 'ethics-sensitive workstreams') are grounded in the project's internal governance structure (Tier 4 implementation_architecture.json ethics_assessment: C13, C14) rather than in quoted HE regulatory provisions. 'Administrative eligibility declarations are handled outside this B.3.2 narrative' explicitly scopes out eligibility compliance assertions. No regulatory claim was found that required but lacked a Tier 1 source reference in traceability_footer.primary_sources[]. The traceability_footer does not include a Tier 1 path, which is appropriate given the absence of regulatory claims requiring Tier 1 citation.

section13_checks.prohibition_description: Producing outputs in Tier 5 that are not traceable to specific inputs in Tier 1–4.
section13_checks.check_rationale: Applicable to Tier 5 artifacts. The artifact declares traceability_footer.no_unsupported_claims_declaration: true. Review of all 15 claim_statuses entries confirms none carry status 'unresolved' with a missing source_ref; all 15 carry either 'confirmed' or 'inferred' status, each with a stated source_ref pointing to a Tier 2B, Tier 3, or Tier 4 path. The traceability_footer.primary_sources[] provides a comprehensive set of 10 named source references across Tier 2 (2A and 2B), Tier 3, and Tier 4. No unsupported claim (one with no source_ref and unresolved status) was identified. The no_unsupported_claims_declaration is consistent with the claim_statuses evidence.

section13_checks.prohibition_description: Modifying Tier 1 or Tier 2 source documents to reflect project-specific assumptions.
section13_checks.check_rationale: Not checkable from a deliverable artifact. This prohibition applies to write operations against Tier 1 and Tier 2 source files, which cannot be detected by reading a Tier 5 output artifact. The implementation_section.json is a Tier 5 deliverable that reads from, but does not write to, Tier 1 or Tier 2 source documents. Check recorded as pass per skill specification §2 non-applicability rule.

section13_checks.prohibition_description: Treating CLAUDE.md as advisory or optional.
section13_checks.check_rationale: Not directly checkable from a deliverable artifact. This prohibition applies to the operational behaviour of agents, skills, and workflows, not to the content of a proposal section deliverable. The implementation_section.json artifact does not contain statements that characterise CLAUDE.md as optional or advisory. Check recorded as pass per skill specification §2 non-applicability rule.
